artifact_name_zh: 弓形器
ocr_text:
弓形器為適應戰車需要的一種駕馭馬匹的器具，固定在御者腰帶上，做作為挂勒韁繩之用，以利騰出雙手，持兵器作戰或執弓射箭。
The implement, fastened to the waist of the driver-warrior for securing the reins during the battles, frees up both hands to hold weapons.
弓形器
Bow-shaped Implement
caption: 弓形器使用方式圖解，繪御者腰繫弓形器駕馭戰車。

artifact_name_zh: 劍（劍的部位名稱與越王州勾劍）
ocr_text:
首 Pommel 莖(劍夾) Hilt 縱 Cord Grip 后 Collar 鐔(劍弭) Sheath Guard 珌(劍鼻) Hanging Loop 削(劍室) Sheath 鍔 Blade 鋒 Tip 摽 Chape
戉(越) Yue 王 Wang 州勾 Zhou gou
(King Zhou-gou of the Yue State)
春秋戰國之交，東南方的吳越諸國崛起，以其精銳兵器，橫掃中原。此劍為越王州勾(文獻作朱勾，公元前448-441)所製的鋒利兵器。傳世尚有多把越王劍，可以驗證古代干將、莫邪鑄劍的傳說。
During the turn of the Spring and Autumn and Warring States periods, southern states Wu and Yue grew powerful and claimed supremacy with their finely cast weapons. The sharp sword was commissioned by King Zhou-gou (448-441 B.C.E.) of the Yue State. Many of his swords have survived until today and attest to the legends of the two famous mythical masters of sword casting: Gan-jiang and Mo-ye.
劍 Sword
caption: 劍的部位名稱圖解，並介紹越王州勾劍及其銘文。
notes: 圖解兼介紹牌，左下有銅器一角。

artifact_name_zh: 蔡侯產戈及其錯金銘文／弩機
artifact_name_en: Ge Dagger of Marquis Chan of Cai and inscription of gold inlay / Crossbow
ocr_text:
戈 Ge (dagger)
蔡處南方，於春秋中晚期亦盛行鳥蟲篆書。此劍銘曰「蔡侯產之用戈」，知為公元前471至457年間所鑄器。
During the mid to late Spring and Autumn period, the calligraphic style miao-chong-zhuan (script resembling the shapes of animals) was popular in the southern state Cai. The six-character sword inscription reads that it belonged to Marquis Chan of Cai, dating it to between 471-457 B.C.E.
蔡侯產戈及其錯金銘文
Ge Dagger of Marquis Chan of Cai and inscription of gold inlay
1.懸刀 Trigger 2.望山 Viewing Hole 3.牛和牙 Lock Plate 4.弦 Bowstring 5.箭 Arrow
望山 Viewing Hole 箭的飛行線 Arrow Trapezory 瞄準線 Aiming Line 目標 Target
弩機 Crossbow
caption: 蔡侯產戈及其錯金銘文展品與弩機原理圖解。
notes: 右側展出戈本體（嵌金銘文），下方為弩機構造圖解。

artifact_name_zh: 帶鉤
ocr_text:
帶鉤，約始於春秋而盛於戰國。質材多樣、鑲嵌多方，在貴族的衣帶上，有精彩的開發：嵌錯金、銀、銅及綠松石等，構成小中見大的細緻景觀。
Belt hooks appeared in the Spring and Autumn period and turned very popular during the Warring State period. They are of diverse materials and with all kinds of inlay from gold, silver to bronze and turquoise. A small ornamental addition greatly enhanced the look of belts and ribbons on an aristocrat.
帶鉤
Belt Hooks
caption: 帶鉤展區介紹牌，下繪一件嵌綠松石帶鉤示意。

artifact_name_zh: 銅鏡
ocr_text:
戰國銅鏡紋飾常見有山字紋、菱形紋、連弧紋、羽翅紋、龍紋、夔紋、狩獵紋等，往往承襲自東周銅器紋樣而加以轉化、然後自成系統，是銅鏡發展史上的第一度高峰。
The common patterns decorating the bronze mirrors from the Warring States period include "shan-zi" (a pattern similar to the character "mountain"), lozenges, connected arcs, feathers, dragons, kui dragons, hunting scenes, and so on. Most were the modified versions based upon those on the bronze vessels of Eastern Zhou and then evolved into a system of their own, achieving the first peak in the history of bronze mirrors.
銅鏡
Mirrors
caption: 銅鏡展區介紹牌，下繪一面戰國山字紋銅鏡。

artifact_name_zh: 四夔紋鏡／四山紋鏡／三龍紋鏡

戰國 Warring States Period c. 5th to 3rd century B.C.E.
1.四夔紋鏡 Bronze mirror with four coiling kui dragons pattern
2.四山紋鏡 Bronze mirror with marks in four Chinese characters "mountain"
3.三龍紋鏡 Bronze mirror with three dragons pattern
caption: 展櫃三面戰國銅鏡：四夔紋鏡、四山紋鏡、三龍紋鏡，含名稱牌。
notes: 展品編號②①③，名稱牌細字部分模糊。

artifact_name_zh: 八吉祥圖卡
ocr_text:
寶傘 護佑眾生 克服恐懼
雙魚 幸福自在 忠貞不渝
寶瓶 圓滿無漏 增添財富
妙蓮 潔白無瑕 盡善盡美
右旋白螺 驍勇勝利 好運常在
吉祥結 佛智圓滿 長壽永恆
勝利幢 戰勝邪魔 除障降魔
法輪 善慧成就 得大自在
國立故宮博物院 TALES
caption: 八吉祥（寶傘、雙魚、寶瓶、妙蓮、白螺、吉祥結、勝利幢、法輪）圖卡立牌。
notes: 故宮 x TALES 文創解說卡，非青銅展品。
